name.description:
Name of your organization or company, in lowercase. Used in documentation.
The generated client name defaults to this. To customize the capitalization,
use `custom_casings`.
name.markdownDescription:
Name of your organization or company, in lowercase. Used in documentation.
The generated client name defaults to this. To customize the capitalization,
use `custom_casings`.

contact.description: Contact email for bug reports, questions, and support requests.
contact.markdownDescription: Contact email for bug reports, questions, and support requests.

security_contact.description:
Security email address for reporting vulnerabilities. Will be mentioned in the generated
SECURITY.md. Defaults to the contact email.
security_contact.markdownDescription:
Security email address for reporting vulnerabilities. Will be mentioned in the generated
SECURITY.md. Defaults to the contact email.

security_policy_url.description: Link to a page covering your security policy. Will be mentioned in the generated SECURITY.md.
security_policy_url.markdownDescription: Link to a page covering your security policy. Will be mentioned in the generated SECURITY.md.

security_policy_terms.description:
Terms for your security policy. Will be added to the generated SECURITY.md.

Stainless always generates a default security policy covering SDKs security issues.
This field is for additional terms you want to add and will be included in a section
'[Your org name] Terms and Policies'.
security_policy_terms.markdownDescription:
Terms for your security policy. Will be added to the generated SECURITY.md.

Stainless always generates a default security policy covering SDKs security issues.
This field is for additional terms you want to add and will be included in a section
'[Your org name] Terms and Policies'.

github_org.description: Name of the GitHub organization that the production SDKs live in.
github_org.markdownDescription: Name of the GitHub organization that the production SDKs live in.

upload_spec.description:
Whether or not to enable uploading the input openapi spec and publicly exposing it in the SDK.

This allows running tests in the SDKs against a mock server, and if disabled will disable tests from automatically running in CI.

You can optionally add a branch target, such as `AcmeOrg/acme-typescript#master`. By default, the target branch is `main`.

keep_files.description:
An array of file paths, relative to the root of the SDK repository, that will not be deleted during codegen.  Generally we recommend you use [custom code](/docs/sdks/configure/custom-code) instead, but `keep_files` can be useful for other files you manually add that need to persist through codegen.

For example:

~~~yaml
keep_files:
  - .github/workflows/ci_tests.yml
~~~

Cloudflare account. This is useful if you want to easily set up an MCP server that handles

See https://www.stainless.com/docs/guides/generate-an-mcp-server#deploy-a-remote-mcp-server for more information.

node_migration.description:
If you're migrating from a `node` target to `typescript` you can specify 2 version strings that will be used in the migration guides and scripts.

The versions will be used verbatim, such as in "Run migrations from your-sdk v{previous_version} to v{migrated_version}".

If your SDK is post-1.0, we recommend to only include the major version, such as `previous_version: "2", migrated_version: "3"`.

If you're migrating from a `node` target to `typescript` you can specify 2 version strings that will be used in the migration guides and scripts.

The versions will be used verbatim, such as in "Run migrations from your-sdk v{previous_version} to v{migrated_version}".

If your SDK is post-1.0, we recommend to only include the major version, such as `previous_version: "2", migrated_version: "3"`.

use_uv.description: In the python.2025-11-20 edition we switched the default package manger from Rye (https://rye.astral.sh) to uv (https://docs.astral.sh/uv/).  This property allows you to control if uv is used regardless of edition.
use_uv.markdownDescription: In the python.2025-11-20 edition we switched the default package manger from Rye (https://rye.astral.sh) to uv (https://docs.astral.sh/uv/).  This property allows you to control if uv is used regardless of edition.

use_eager_iterables.description:
When enabled, codegen uses a custom `EagerIterable` type instead of a plain `Iterable`.

By default, Pydantic consumes `Iterable` fields lazily and does not validate items upfront, which can cause
data to be silently lost on repeated `model_dump()` calls[0]. `EagerIterable` eagerly consumes the input,
validates all items, and preserves the original container type at runtime (e.g. a `set` stays a `set`).
Serialization (e.g. `model_dump()`) always produces a `list`.

[0]: https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic/issues/9541
use_eager_iterables.markdownDescription:
When enabled, codegen uses a custom `EagerIterable` type instead of a plain `Iterable`.

By default, Pydantic consumes `Iterable` fields lazily and does not validate items upfront, which can cause
data to be silently lost on repeated `model_dump()` calls[0]. `EagerIterable` eagerly consumes the input,
validates all items, and preserves the original container type at runtime (e.g. a `set` stays a `set`).
Serialization (e.g. `model_dump()`) always produces a `list`.

[0]: https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic/issues/9541

keep_files.description:
An array of file paths, relative to the root of the SDK repository, that will not be deleted during codegen.  Generally we recommend you use [custom code](/docs/sdks/configure/custom-code) instead, but `keep_files` can be useful for other files you manually add that need to persist through codegen.

For example:

~~~yaml
keep_files:
  - .github/workflows/ci_tests.yml
~~~

package_name.description: The name of the root package, containing the top-level client, options, and methods.
package_name.markdownDescription: The name of the root package, containing the top-level client, options, and methods.

go_module_path_override.description: Override the Go module path used in imports. For example, if your module is hosted at my-custom-host.com/acme/terraform-provider-acme instead of the default {repository.host}/{repository.full_name}. The major version suffix (e.g. `/v2`) is still appended automatically.
go_module_path_override.markdownDescription: Override the Go module path used in imports. For example, if your module is hosted at my-custom-host.com/acme/terraform-provider-acme instead of the default {repository.host}/{repository.full_name}. The major version suffix (e.g. `/v2`) is still appended automatically.

option_package_name.description: Override the name used for the internal `option` package
option_package_name.markdownDescription: Override the name used for the internal `option` package

client_name.description:
Overrides the name used for the API client struct. This name should be pascal cased and a valid public Go identifier. By default, this is `Client`, and the constructor will be called `NewClient`.

Overriding it to e.g. `APIClient` will change the struct to be `APIClient`, and the corresponding constructor will be called `NewAPIClient`.
client_name.markdownDescription:
Overrides the name used for the API client struct. This name should be pascal cased and a valid public Go identifier. By default, this is `Client`, and the constructor will be called `NewClient`.

Overriding it to e.g. `APIClient` will change the struct to be `APIClient`, and the corresponding constructor will be called `NewAPIClient`.

go_language_version.description: Explicitly set the Go version to use
go_language_version.markdownDescription: Explicitly set the Go version to use

anyOf.description: Enable v2 of the Go SDK generator.
anyOf.markdownDescription: Enable v2 of the Go SDK generator.

anyOf.description: Enable v2 of the Go SDK generator, with some special handling for upgrading from v1. This option should only be used if you previously published a Go SDK with v1.
anyOf.markdownDescription: Enable v2 of the Go SDK generator, with some special handling for upgrading from v1. This option should only be used if you previously published a Go SDK with v1.

anyOf.description: Enable v1 of the Go SDK generator.
anyOf.markdownDescription: Enable v1 of the Go SDK generator.

enable_v2.description:
Enable the V2 Go SDK generator with "true" or "migration". **Caution:** the V2 SDK is not backwards compatible with V1.

Defaults to 'false' if not specified.
enable_v2.markdownDescription:
Enable the V2 Go SDK generator with "true" or "migration". **Caution:** the V2 SDK is not backwards compatible with V1.

Defaults to 'false' if not specified.

union_property_getters.description: Generates getter methods to access nested properties in unions without needing to switch on the variant.
union_property_getters.markdownDescription: Generates getter methods to access nested properties in unions without needing to switch on the variant.

subtle_union_naming.description: Doesn't suffix union types with '-Union', and removes the 'Of-' prefix from union variant names. This option is **not recommended** for large (or soon-to-be large) APIs, as it comes with visual clarity and forwards compatibility costs.
subtle_union_naming.markdownDescription: Doesn't suffix union types with '-Union', and removes the 'Of-' prefix from union variant names. This option is **not recommended** for large (or soon-to-be large) APIs, as it comes with visual clarity and forwards compatibility costs.

remove_params_suffix.description: Doesn't suffix params types with '-Param'. Use with caution: this means models cannot be used in both params and response (without additional configuration). Currently available for Go V2 only.
remove_params_suffix.markdownDescription: Doesn't suffix params types with '-Param'. Use with caution: this means models cannot be used in both params and response (without additional configuration). Currently available for Go V2 only.

path_param_encoding.description: Setting to false will not automatically apply URI encoding to path parameters.
path_param_encoding.markdownDescription: Setting to false will not automatically apply URI encoding to path parameters.

escape_html_in_json.description: In the Go Standard Library HTML in JSON is escaped by default, so this defaults to true.
escape_html_in_json.markdownDescription: In the Go Standard Library HTML in JSON is escaped by default, so this defaults to true.

nullable_map_items.description:
Generate pointers for nullable values inside maps and arrays (e.g. `map[string]*string` instead of `map[string]string`, `[]*string` instead of `[]string`).

Go V2 only — Go V3 always applies this behavior. Defaults to `true` starting with the `go.2026-04-15` edition.
nullable_map_items.markdownDescription:
Generate pointers for nullable values inside maps and arrays (e.g. `map[string]*string` instead of `map[string]string`, `[]*string` instead of `[]string`).

Go V2 only — Go V3 always applies this behavior. Defaults to `true` starting with the `go.2026-04-15` edition.

back_compat_options_field_name.description: Setting to `false` makes the `options` field in the client / service structs private
back_compat_options_field_name.markdownDescription: Setting to `false` makes the `options` field in the client / service structs private

production_repo.description:
The production repo that publishes to the Terraform Registry.

The Terraform Registry requires GitHub repos to be prefixed with `terraform-provider-<providername>`, e.g. `Acme-Org/terraform-provider-acme`.
production_repo.markdownDescription:
The production repo that publishes to the Terraform Registry.

The Terraform Registry requires GitHub repos to be prefixed with `terraform-provider-<providername>`, e.g. `Acme-Org/terraform-provider-acme`.

timeouts.description: whether to enable a timeout property for resources

default.description:
Default timeout for the resource and subresources' Terraform operations in milliseconds or ISO8601.

You can override this in subresources or individual methods.

This doesn't affect the request-level timeout.
default.markdownDescription:
Default timeout for the resource and subresources' Terraform operations in milliseconds or ISO8601.

You can override this in subresources or individual methods.

This doesn't affect the request-level timeout.

timeouts.markdownDescription: whether to enable a timeout property for resources

collection_property_name.markdownDescription: Attribute name for the result collection for list data sources (default: `items`)

keep_files.description:
An array of file paths, relative to the root of the SDK repository, that will not be deleted during codegen.  Generally we recommend you use [custom code](/docs/sdks/configure/custom-code) instead, but `keep_files` can be useful for other files you manually add that need to persist through codegen.

For example:

~~~yaml
keep_files:
  - .github/workflows/ci_tests.yml
~~~

An array of file paths, relative to the root of the SDK repository, that will not be deleted during codegen.  Generally we recommend you use [custom code](/docs/sdks/configure/custom-code) instead, but `keep_files` can be useful for other files you manually add that need to persist through codegen.

For example:

~~~yaml

  - .github/workflows/ci_tests.yml
~~~

For example, if your module is hosted at my-custom-host.com/acme/terraform-provider-acme instead of the default {repository.host}/{repository.full_name}. The major version suffix (e.g. `/v2`) is still appended automatically.

default_format.description:
The default output format for CLI methods. This can be overridden on each
endpoint in its `cli.format` option or with the `--format` flag when
running the command.
default_format.markdownDescription:
The default output format for CLI methods. This can be overridden on each
endpoint in its `cli.format` option or with the `--format` flag when
running the command.

default_json_get_format.description:
The default output format for CLI GET endpoints that return JSON (excludes
binary responses). When set, this overrides `default_format` for those
endpoints. This can be overridden on each endpoint in its `cli.format`
option or with the `--format` flag when running the command.
default_json_get_format.markdownDescription:
The default output format for CLI GET endpoints that return JSON (excludes
binary responses). When set, this overrides `default_format` for those
endpoints. This can be overridden on each endpoint in its `cli.format`
option or with the `--format` flag when running the command.

default_error_format.description:
The default output format for CLI error responses. This can be overridden
on each endpoint in its `cli.error_format` option or with the
`--format-error` flag when running the command.
default_error_format.markdownDescription:
The default output format for CLI error responses. This can be overridden
on each endpoint in its `cli.error_format` option or with the
`--format-error` flag when running the command.

package_name.description:
The name of the root package, containing the top-level client, options, and methods.

For example, `package_name: acme` would result in `use Acme\Client` for SDK users.

Unlike the composer_package_name, this name is only used **within the SDK library code**.
package_name.markdownDescription:
The name of the root package, containing the top-level client, options, and methods.

For example, `package_name: acme` would result in `use Acme\Client` for SDK users.

Unlike the composer_package_name, this name is only used **within the SDK library code**.

composer_package_name.description: The ecosystem name of the package as used by Composer & Packagist. The name should resemble 'acme/acme-php'.

environments.description:
Map of the name of the environment which appears in the SDK to the corresponding url to use.
The first environment in the map will be used as the default environment.

~~~yaml
environments:
  production: https://example.com/api
  sandbox: https://sandbox.example.com/api
~~~

resources.description: Resources define the organization for your API and the endpoints that are available under which resources.

$shared.description: Used to declare any models that should be shared across resources. Defining methods in this resource is not supported.
$shared.markdownDescription: Used to declare any models that should be shared across resources. Defining methods in this resource is not supported.

$client.description: Used to define methods and resources at the client level, e.g. for an API status endpoint
$client.markdownDescription: Used to define methods and resources at the client level, e.g. for an API status endpoint

additionalProperties.description: Defines a resource and models for your API: https://www.stainless.com/docs/guides/configure#resources
additionalProperties.markdownDescription: Defines a resource and models for your API: https://www.stainless.com/docs/guides/configure#resources

resources.markdownDescription: Resources define the organization for your API and the endpoints that are available under which resources.

endpoint.description: Endpoint to use as the example method, in the format `post /foo/bar/{id}`
endpoint.markdownDescription: Endpoint to use as the example method, in the format `post /foo/bar/{id}`

params.description:
The request params to include in the example.

e.g.

params:
  query_param: 'foo'
  body_param: true
  path_param: id
params.markdownDescription:
The request params to include in the example.

e.g.

params:
  query_param: 'foo'
  body_param: true
  path_param: id

response_property.description: The property on the response to log, e.g. `id` on a `get /accounts` endpoints adds a `console.log(account.id)` or equivalent in the example
response_property.markdownDescription: The property on the response to log, e.g. `id` on a `get /accounts` endpoints adds a `console.log(account.id)` or equivalent in the example

assign_to.description:
The name of the variable to assign the result of this request to for this example.

For example, given the method `post /v1/customers`, set `assign_to: customer` to produce example code like this:

~~~ts
customer = client.customers.create();
~~~
assign_to.markdownDescription:
The name of the variable to assign the result of this request to for this example.

For example, given the method `post /v1/customers`, set `assign_to: customer` to produce example code like this:

~~~ts

~~~

errors.description:
Example of how to handle errors in the SDK.

NOTE: Even though this is an "error" example, it should be well-formed! (E.g. it shouldn't have missing or invalid parameters.)
It demonstrates how to handle network errors and the like, not how to handle malformed requests, since the SDK ensures that requests are well-formed.

We suggest you use the most standard endpoint for the `default` example, and the most important example for the `headline` example

per_endpoint_security.description:
When `true`, the `security` field on an operation will be respected and the SDK will only
send matching security schemes.

For example:

```yaml
security: [{ BearerAuth: [] }, { APIKeyAuth }]
paths:
  /accounts:
    post:
      security: [{ BearerAuth: [] }]
```

By default, if the SDK is given the client option for the `APIKeyAuth` scheme, then it will happily send it.

When `per_endpoint_security` is set to `true`, the SDK will *only* try and send the `BearerAuth` header.
per_endpoint_security.markdownDescription:
When `true`, the `security` field on an operation will be respected and the SDK will only
send matching security schemes.

For example:

```yaml
security: [{ BearerAuth: [] }, { APIKeyAuth }]
paths:
  /accounts:
    post:
      security: [{ BearerAuth: [] }]
```

By default, if the SDK is given the client option for the `APIKeyAuth` scheme, then it will happily send it.

When `per_endpoint_security` is set to `true`, the SDK will *only* try and send the `BearerAuth` header.

file_header.description:
Custom header to add at the top of all generated SDK files.

The header will be added as a comment at the very top of each generated file,
before the "File generated from our OpenAPI spec by Stainless" line.
Each language will use its appropriate comment syntax automatically.

Example usage for copyright headers:
~~~yaml
settings:
  file_header: |
    Copyright (c) 2025 ACME Corporation.
    All rights reserved.

    This source code is licensed under the terms described in the LICENSE file in
    the root directory of this source tree.
~~~

The above will generate (in TypeScript):
~~~ts
// Copyright (c) 2025 ACME Corporation.
// All rights reserved.
//
// This source code is licensed under the terms described in the LICENSE file in
// the root directory of this source tree.
// File generated from our OpenAPI spec by Stainless. See CONTRIBUTING.md for details.
~~~
file_header.markdownDescription:
Custom header to add at the top of all generated SDK files.

The header will be added as a comment at the very top of each generated file,
before the "File generated from our OpenAPI spec by Stainless" line.
Each language will use its appropriate comment syntax automatically.

Example usage for copyright headers:
~~~yaml
settings:
  file_header: |
    Copyright (c) 2025 ACME Corporation.
    All rights reserved.

    This source code is licensed under the terms described in the LICENSE file in
    the root directory of this source tree.
~~~

The above will generate (in TypeScript):
~~~ts
// Copyright (c) 2025 ACME Corporation.
// All rights reserved.
//
// This source code is licensed under the terms described in the LICENSE file in
// the root directory of this source tree.
// File generated from our OpenAPI spec by Stainless. See CONTRIBUTING.md for details.
~~~

suggest_all_potential_models.markdownDescription: Suggest every schema that could be a model as Model/Recommended, not just those that are repeated

nested_format.description:
Configures how query parameters with nested objects render in the query string. Accepted values are `brackets` (the default) and `dots`.

Example object: `{ a: { b: "c" } }`

- brackets syntax -> `a[b]=c`
- dot syntax -> `a.b=c`
nested_format.markdownDescription:
Configures how query parameters with nested objects render in the query string. Accepted values are `brackets` (the default) and `dots`.

Example object: `{ a: { b: "c" } }`

- brackets syntax -> `a[b]=c`
- dot syntax -> `a.b=c`

array_format.description:
Configures how query parameters with array values render in the query string. Accepted values are `brackets`, `comma` (the default), and `repeat`.

Example object: `{ in: ["foo", "bar"] }`

- brackets syntax -> `in[]=foo&in[]=bar`
- comma syntax -> `in=foo,bar`
- repeat syntax -> `in=foo&in=bar`
array_format.markdownDescription:
Configures how query parameters with array values render in the query string. Accepted values are `brackets`, `comma` (the default), and `repeat`.

Example object: `{ in: ["foo", "bar"] }`

- brackets syntax -> `in[]=foo&in[]=bar`
- comma syntax -> `in=foo,bar`
- repeat syntax -> `in=foo&in=bar`

array_format.description:
Configures how arrays are serialized in multipart/form-data request bodies. Accepted values are `brackets`, `comma`, `repeat`, and `indices`.

When not set, each language SDK uses its existing default:
- TypeScript, Python: `brackets`
- Ruby, PHP: `repeat`
- Go v1: `indices` (dot-separated)
- Java, Kotlin, C#, Go v2: inherits from `query_settings.array_format`

Example array: `{ items: ["foo", "bar"] }`

- brackets syntax -> `items[]=foo&items[]=bar`
- comma syntax -> `items=foo,bar`
- repeat syntax -> `items=foo&items=bar`
- indices syntax -> `items[0]=foo&items[1]=bar`
array_format.markdownDescription:
Configures how arrays are serialized in multipart/form-data request bodies. Accepted values are `brackets`, `comma`, `repeat`, and `indices`.

When not set, each language SDK uses its existing default:
- TypeScript, Python: `brackets`
- Ruby, PHP: `repeat`
- Go v1: `indices` (dot-separated)
- Java, Kotlin, C#, Go v2: inherits from `query_settings.array_format`

Example array: `{ items: ["foo", "bar"] }`

- brackets syntax -> `items[]=foo&items[]=bar`
- comma syntax -> `items=foo,bar`
- repeat syntax -> `items=foo&items=bar`
- indices syntax -> `items[0]=foo&items[1]=bar`

server_variable.description:
Links this client option to a variable in the url configured by `environments`.

~~~yaml
environments:
  production: https://{region}.acme.com
~~~

Then `server_variable: "region"` will mean the value of the `{region}` will be replaced by the value of this client option.

Links this client option to a variable in the url configured by `environments`.

~~~yaml

~~~

Then `server_variable: "region"` will mean the value of the `{region}` will be replaced by the value of this client option.

send_as_query_param.markdownDescription: Use this client opt to fill out the query parameters in requests.

send_as_body_param.description: Use this client opt to fill out the body parameters in requests.
send_as_body_param.markdownDescription: Use this client opt to fill out the body parameters in requests.

send_as_path_param.description: Use this client opt to fill out the path parameters in requests. Note that the path parameter is required to make the request, and should either be provided either on the client or on the request if the requests requires it.
send_as_path_param.markdownDescription: Use this client opt to fill out the path parameters in requests. Note that the path parameter is required to make the request, and should either be provided either on the client or on the request if the requests requires it.

required_in_tests.markdownDescription: Whether to provide this argument in unit tests. Set to true if this argument is required for internal request tests to run successfully

Extra arguments that the client accepts, such as an API key.

Learn more: https://www.stainless.com/docs/configure/client#client-opts

default_client_example_name.description:
The default name to use for the generated example client instantiated variable. If not set, defaults to 'client'.

This is used in the generated examples, e.g. `const client = new Acme();`.
default_client_example_name.markdownDescription:
The default name to use for the generated example client instantiated variable. If not set, defaults to 'client'.

This is used in the generated examples, e.g. `const client = new Acme();`.

default_client_name.description: The default name to use for the generated client class(es). If not set, defaults to the organization name.
default_client_name.markdownDescription: The default name to use for the generated client class(es). If not set, defaults to the organization name.

default_env_prefix.description:
The default prefix to use for environment variables, e.g. `ACME` -> `ACME_BASE_URL`. If not set, defaults to the organization name.

Note this is only used for cases where Stainless generates an environment variable name, if you have an explicit environment variable set, e.g.
in `client_settings.opts.read_env`, this env prefix will **not** apply.
default_env_prefix.markdownDescription:
The default prefix to use for environment variables, e.g. `ACME` -> `ACME_BASE_URL`. If not set, defaults to the organization name.

Note this is only used for cases where Stainless generates an environment variable name, if you have an explicit environment variable set, e.g.
in `client_settings.opts.read_env`, this env prefix will **not** apply.

header.description: Header to send the idempotency token in
header.markdownDescription: Header to send the idempotency token in

omit_stainless_headers.description:
Do not send [`X-Stainless-*` headers](https://www.stainless.com/docs/configure/client/#default-headers) with each request.

This can be useful if your API has a headers allowlist.
omit_stainless_headers.markdownDescription:
Do not send [`X-Stainless-*` headers](https://www.stainless.com/docs/configure/client/#default-headers) with each request.

This can be useful if your API has a headers allowlist.

default_headers.description: Headers sent with every API request
default_headers.markdownDescription: Headers sent with every API request

default_timeout.description: Configure the [default timeout for client calls](https://www.stainless.com/docs/configure/client#timeouts), in milliseconds or ISO 8601 (default is 60 seconds)
default_timeout.markdownDescription: Configure the [default timeout for client calls](https://www.stainless.com/docs/configure/client#timeouts), in milliseconds or ISO 8601 (default is 60 seconds)

max_retries.description: Maximum number of times to retry for all endpoints. Use 0 to disable retries.
max_retries.markdownDescription: Maximum number of times to retry for all endpoints. Use 0 to disable retries.

initial_delay_seconds.description: Seconds to wait before the first retry.
initial_delay_seconds.markdownDescription: Seconds to wait before the first retry.

max_delay_seconds.description: We increase the retry time with exponential backoff, each retry taking twice as long as the last, until this value of max seconds to retry.
max_delay_seconds.markdownDescription: We increase the retry time with exponential backoff, each retry taking twice as long as the last, until this value of max seconds to retry.

keepalive_expiry.description: Time limit on idle keep-alive connections in seconds
keepalive_expiry.markdownDescription: Time limit on idle keep-alive connections in seconds

default_http_limits.description: Default resource limits for the HTTP client. See https://www.python-httpx.org/advanced/resource-limits for defaults and more information.
default_http_limits.markdownDescription: Default resource limits for the HTTP client. See https://www.python-httpx.org/advanced/resource-limits for defaults and more information.

client_settings.description: Settings for customizing the [Client class](https://www.stainless.com/docs/configure/client) in the SDKs.
client_settings.markdownDescription: Settings for customizing the [Client class](https://www.stainless.com/docs/configure/client) in the SDKs.

pagination.description:
Defines pagination schemes for pagination helpers and auto-pagination in the SDKs.

See [our pagination guide](/docs/sdks/configure/pagination) for more details and examples.

continue_on_empty_items.description: If set to true, the auto pagination helpers will continue to fetch pages even if there are no items returned, and will only stop when it is impossible to fetch another page
continue_on_empty_items.markdownDescription: If set to true, the auto pagination helpers will continue to fetch pages even if there are no items returned, and will only stop when it is impossible to fetch another page

unspecified_endpoints.description: List of endpoints to explicitly ignore, such as `["post /internal_endpoint", "get /redundant_endpoint"]`

release_environment.description: GitHub environment to run the release workflow in.

publish_packages.description: Whether publishing to package managers should be included in GitHub automations.

reviewers.description: List of default reviewers to assign to a customer release PR

code_owners.description:
Github CODEOWNERS file content to add to SDK repositories

Example:

      '*': ['@acme/sdk-team-1']
      'src/**': ['@acme/sdk-team-2']

detect_breaking_changes.description:
Enable to automatically detect some breaking changes.

With `detect_breaking_changes` enabled, we'll check out the public API test files in their pre-release state and attempt to compile/lint them against your new SDK version.  If your previous test code fails to compile with your new SDK, we surface the errors as an indicator that your new release contains breaking changes.
detect_breaking_changes.markdownDescription:
Enable to automatically detect some breaking changes.

With `detect_breaking_changes` enabled, we'll check out the public API test files in their pre-release state and attempt to compile/lint them against your new SDK version.  If your previous test code fails to compile with your new SDK, we surface the errors as an indicator that your new release contains breaking changes.

codeflow.description: Configures various codeflow (automated releases to package managers) options.
codeflow.markdownDescription: Configures various codeflow (automated releases to package managers) options.

propertyNames.description: A key to use as an internal representation of this word. Should only consist of lowercase, alphanumeric characters.
propertyNames.markdownDescription: A key to use as an internal representation of this word. Should only consist of lowercase, alphanumeric characters.

initialism.description:
If set to true, the term is used as an initialism: treated as an abbreviation of initial letters (for example, "CPU" and "API").
If set to false, the term is removed from the set of initialisms.

By default we use `[3ds, ach, acl, ai, api, ats, cpu, crm, db, dns, e2e, eeoc, gb, gpu, hd, hris, html, http, https, id, ip, iso, jsonl, kb, kyb, kyc, lfs, mb, mp3, mp4, nft, saml, sdk, sku, sms, ss, ssh, sso, url]` as initialisms.

Example:

~~~yaml
custom_casings:
  mcp: { initialism: true }
~~~
initialism.markdownDescription:
If set to true, the term is used as an initialism: treated as an abbreviation of initial letters (for example, "CPU" and "API").
If set to false, the term is removed from the set of initialisms.

By default we use `[3ds, ach, acl, ai, api, ats, cpu, crm, db, dns, e2e, eeoc, gb, gpu, hd, hris, html, http, https, id, ip, iso, jsonl, kb, kyb, kyc, lfs, mb, mp3, mp4, nft, saml, sdk, sku, sms, ss, ssh, sso, url]` as initialisms.

Example:

~~~yaml
custom_casings:
  mcp: { initialism: true }
~~~

snake.description:
Term rendered with lowercase characters separated by underscores.

Example:

~~~yaml
custom_casings:
  payment_method:
    snake: "payment_method"
~~~

custom_casings.description: Defines how phrases are rendered in different SDKs. Can be either an 'initialism' which tells the generator to treat the word as an abbreviation like 'API' or 'CPU', or a more powerful `CasingConfig` which can specify how that word is represented in the different casing formats. In rare cases where customization of pluralization is required, you can also specify singular/plural versions of the word.
custom_casings.markdownDescription: Defines how phrases are rendered in different SDKs. Can be either an 'initialism' which tells the generator to treat the word as an abbreviation like 'API' or 'CPU', or a more powerful `CasingConfig` which can specify how that word is represented in the different casing formats. In rare cases where customization of pluralization is required, you can also specify singular/plural versions of the word.

openapi.description: Describes changes to the input OpenAPI spec and whether or not to add code samples to the output OpenAPI spec. See [our transforms guide](/docs/openapi/transforms) for more details.

transforms.description:
List of transforms to apply to the OpenAPI spec before SDK generation. See [our transforms reference](/docs/openapi/transforms/reference) for available commands.

Note: `openapi.transforms` was named `openapi.transformations` in early versions of Stainless.  The names are interchangeable, but we recommend you use the newer `openapi.transforms`.

matches_schema.description: JSONPath to template schema. Finds all schemas that deep-equal this one.
matches_schema.markdownDescription: JSONPath to template schema. Finds all schemas that deep-equal this one.

ignore.description: Path(s) to exclude from matching (e.g., the template itself).
ignore.markdownDescription: Path(s) to exclude from matching (e.g., the template itself).

target.description: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to the value(s) to replace. Can be a string, array of strings, or array mixing strings with matches_schema queries.
target.markdownDescription: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to the value(s) to replace. Can be a string, array of strings, or array mixing strings with matches_schema queries.

value.description: The new value to set at the target location(s).
value.markdownDescription: The new value to set at the target location(s).

template.description: If true, enables {{value}} substitution in string values. Use for appending/prepending to strings. Example: "{{value}}\nAdditional notes" or "[BETA] {{value}}". Requires value to contain {{value}} and target to be a string.
template.markdownDescription: If true, enables {{value}} substitution in string values. Use for appending/prepending to strings. Example: "{{value}}\nAdditional notes" or "[BETA] {{value}}". Requires value to contain {{value}} and target to be a string.

args.description:
Replace values at existing paths. Fails if path doesn't exist.

Supports {{value}} templating for string manipulation. Supports matches_schema for finding duplicate schemas.

Examples:
~~~yaml
# Fix incorrect type with filter
- command: update
  reason: "Account IDs are strings"
  args:
    target: '$.paths..parameters[?(@.name == "account_id")].schema.type'
    value: "string"

# String templating: append text
- command: update
  reason: "Add deprecation notice"
  args:
    target: "$.components.schemas.LegacyUser.description"
    value: "{{value}}\n\nDEPRECATED: Use NewUser instead"
    template: true

# Schema matching: replace duplicates
- command: update
  reason: "Replace duplicate error schemas with $ref"
  args:
    target:
      - matches_schema: $.components.schemas.ErrorTemplate
        ignore: $.components.schemas.ErrorTemplate
    value:
      $ref: "#/components/schemas/Error"
~~~

matches_schema.description: JSONPath to template schema. Finds all schemas that deep-equal this one.
matches_schema.markdownDescription: JSONPath to template schema. Finds all schemas that deep-equal this one.

ignore.description: Path(s) to exclude from matching (e.g., the template itself).
ignore.markdownDescription: Path(s) to exclude from matching (e.g., the template itself).

target.description: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to array(s) or object(s) to append to. Can be a string, array of strings, or array mixing strings with matches_schema queries.
target.markdownDescription: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to array(s) or object(s) to append to. Can be a string, array of strings, or array mixing strings with matches_schema queries.

value.description: For arrays: item(s) to push. For objects: properties to add (fails if property already exists).
value.markdownDescription: For arrays: item(s) to push. For objects: properties to add (fails if property already exists).

args.description:
Add items to arrays or add new properties to objects. Attempting to add a property that already exists will fail. This alerts you when the spec is fixed.

Use for temporary fixes. For intentional overrides, use merge.

Examples:
~~~yaml
# Add to array
- command: append
  reason: "Add id to required fields"
  args:
    target: "$.components.schemas.User.required"
    value: "id"

# Add property (fails if exists)
- command: append
  reason: "API spec missing id field, adding temporarily"
  args:
    target: "$.components.schemas.User.properties"
    value:
      id:
        type: "string"
        format: "uuid"
~~~

matches_schema.description: JSONPath to template schema. Finds all schemas that deep-equal this one.
matches_schema.markdownDescription: JSONPath to template schema. Finds all schemas that deep-equal this one.

ignore.description: Path(s) to exclude from matching (e.g., the template itself).
ignore.markdownDescription: Path(s) to exclude from matching (e.g., the template itself).

target.description: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to object(s) to merge into. Can be a string, array of strings, or array mixing strings with matches_schema queries.
target.markdownDescription: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to object(s) to merge into. Can be a string, array of strings, or array mixing strings with matches_schema queries.

value.description: The object to deep merge into the target location(s). Properties from this object will be merged into existing properties.
value.markdownDescription: The object to deep merge into the target location(s). Properties from this object will be merged into existing properties.

args.description:
Merge an object into targets. Overwrites existing properties. Uses deep merge, so nested objects are merged recursively.

Use for permanent overrides like x-stainless-naming or x-stainless-skip. For temporary fixes, use append.

Examples:
~~~yaml
# Add x-stainless extension
- command: merge
  reason: "Terraform doesn't need pagination params"
  args:
    target: '$..parameters[?(@.name == "limit")]'
    value:
      schema:
        x-stainless-skip: ["terraform"]

# Set language-specific naming
- command: merge
  reason: "Avoid Python parameter conflict"
  args:
    target: "$.components.schemas.Request.properties.timeout"
    value:
      x-stainless-naming:
        python:
          method_argument: "api_timeout"
~~~

target.description: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to the node(s) to remove. Can be a string, array of strings, or array mixing strings with matches_schema queries.
target.markdownDescription: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to the node(s) to remove. Can be a string, array of strings, or array mixing strings with matches_schema queries.

keys.description: If target points to an object, remove these specific keys from the object. If not provided, removes the entire target node.
keys.markdownDescription: If target points to an object, remove these specific keys from the object. If not provided, removes the entire target node.

args.description:
Delete nodes or specific keys from objects.

Examples:
~~~yaml
# Remove nodes
- command: remove
  reason: "Remove problematic examples"
  args:
    target:
      - "$.paths.*.*.examples"
      - "$.components.schemas.*.example"

# Remove specific keys
- command: remove
  reason: "Remove incorrect default"
  args:
    target: "$.components.schemas.User.properties.role"
    keys: ["default"]

# Remove with filter
- command: remove
  reason: "Remove deprecated parameters"
  args:
    target: '$.paths..parameters[?(@.deprecated == true)]'
~~~

from.description: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to the source value to copy. Must match exactly one node.

to.description: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to the destination(s). Can be a string, array of strings, or array mixing strings with matches_schema queries. If destination doesn't exist, creates it (parent must exist).
to.markdownDescription: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to the destination(s). Can be a string, array of strings, or array mixing strings with matches_schema queries. If destination doesn't exist, creates it (parent must exist).

args.description:
Copy a value to one or more destinations. Creates destination if parent exists. The source value is deep cloned to each destination.

Examples:
~~~yaml
# Copy schema
- command: copy
  reason: "Duplicate User schema"
  args:
    from: "$.components.schemas.User"
    to: "$.components.schemas.Admin"

# Copy to multiple destinations
- command: copy
  reason: "Reuse error schema"
  args:
    from: "$.components.schemas.NotFoundError"
    to:
      - "$.paths./users/{id}.get.responses.404.content.application/json.schema"
      - "$.paths./posts/{id}.get.responses.404.content.application/json.schema"
~~~

from.description: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to the source value to move. Must match exactly one node.
from.markdownDescription: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to the source value to move. Must match exactly one node.

to.description: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to the destination. The parent of the target should exist.
to.markdownDescription: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to the destination. The parent of the target should exist.

args.description:
Move or rename a value from one location to another.

If `from` and `to` share the same parent the node will be renamed. Otherwise it will be relocated.
Only accept a single source and destination target.

Examples:
~~~yaml
# Rename a schema key
- command: move
  reason: "Normalize schema name to PascalCase"
  args:
    from: "$.components.schemas.user_response"
    to: "$.components.schemas.UserResponse"

# Move property between schemas
- command: move
  reason: "Relocate userId from Request to User schema"
  args:
    from: "$.components.schemas.Request.properties.userId"
    to: "$.components.schemas.User.properties.id"
~~~

transforms.markdownDescription:
List of transforms to apply to the OpenAPI spec before SDK generation. See [our transforms reference](/docs/openapi/transforms/reference) for available commands.

Note: `openapi.transforms` was named `openapi.transformations` in early versions of Stainless.  The names are interchangeable, but we recommend you use the newer `openapi.transforms`.

matches_schema.description: JSONPath to template schema. Finds all schemas that deep-equal this one.
matches_schema.markdownDescription: JSONPath to template schema. Finds all schemas that deep-equal this one.

ignore.description: Path(s) to exclude from matching (e.g., the template itself).
ignore.markdownDescription: Path(s) to exclude from matching (e.g., the template itself).

target.description: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to the value(s) to replace. Can be a string, array of strings, or array mixing strings with matches_schema queries.
target.markdownDescription: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to the value(s) to replace. Can be a string, array of strings, or array mixing strings with matches_schema queries.

value.description: The new value to set at the target location(s).
value.markdownDescription: The new value to set at the target location(s).

template.description: If true, enables {{value}} substitution in string values. Use for appending/prepending to strings. Example: "{{value}}\nAdditional notes" or "[BETA] {{value}}". Requires value to contain {{value}} and target to be a string.
template.markdownDescription: If true, enables {{value}} substitution in string values. Use for appending/prepending to strings. Example: "{{value}}\nAdditional notes" or "[BETA] {{value}}". Requires value to contain {{value}} and target to be a string.

args.description:
Replace values at existing paths. Fails if path doesn't exist.

Supports {{value}} templating for string manipulation. Supports matches_schema for finding duplicate schemas.

Examples:
~~~yaml
# Fix incorrect type with filter
- command: update
  reason: "Account IDs are strings"
  args:
    target: '$.paths..parameters[?(@.name == "account_id")].schema.type'
    value: "string"

# String templating: append text
- command: update
  reason: "Add deprecation notice"
  args:
    target: "$.components.schemas.LegacyUser.description"
    value: "{{value}}\n\nDEPRECATED: Use NewUser instead"
    template: true

# Schema matching: replace duplicates
- command: update
  reason: "Replace duplicate error schemas with $ref"
  args:
    target:
      - matches_schema: $.components.schemas.ErrorTemplate
        ignore: $.components.schemas.ErrorTemplate
    value:
      $ref: "#/components/schemas/Error"
~~~

value.description: For arrays: item(s) to push. For objects: properties to add (fails if property already exists).
value.markdownDescription: For arrays: item(s) to push. For objects: properties to add (fails if property already exists).

args.description:
Add items to arrays or add new properties to objects. Attempting to add a property that already exists will fail. This alerts you when the spec is fixed.

Use for temporary fixes. For intentional overrides, use merge.

Examples:
~~~yaml
# Add to array
- command: append
  reason: "Add id to required fields"
  args:
    target: "$.components.schemas.User.required"
    value: "id"

# Add property (fails if exists)
- command: append
  reason: "API spec missing id field, adding temporarily"
  args:
    target: "$.components.schemas.User.properties"
    value:
      id:
        type: "string"
        format: "uuid"
~~~

value.description: The object to deep merge into the target location(s). Properties from this object will be merged into existing properties.

args.description:
Merge an object into targets. Overwrites existing properties. Uses deep merge, so nested objects are merged recursively.

Use for permanent overrides like x-stainless-naming or x-stainless-skip. For temporary fixes, use append.

Examples:
~~~yaml
# Add x-stainless extension
- command: merge
  reason: "Terraform doesn't need pagination params"
  args:
    target: '$..parameters[?(@.name == "limit")]'
    value:
      schema:
        x-stainless-skip: ["terraform"]

# Set language-specific naming
- command: merge
  reason: "Avoid Python parameter conflict"
  args:
    target: "$.components.schemas.Request.properties.timeout"
    value:
      x-stainless-naming:
        python:
          method_argument: "api_timeout"
~~~

Copy a value to one or more destinations. Creates destination if parent exists. The source value is deep cloned to each destination.

Examples:
~~~yaml
# Copy schema
- command: copy
  reason: "Duplicate User schema"
  args:
    from: "$.components.schemas.User"
    to: "$.components.schemas.Admin"

# Copy to multiple destinations
- command: copy
  reason: "Reuse error schema"
  args:
    from: "$.components.schemas.NotFoundError"
    to:
      - "$.paths./users/{id}.get.responses.404.content.application/json.schema"
      - "$.paths./posts/{id}.get.responses.404.content.application/json.schema"
~~~

from.description: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to the source value to move. Must match exactly one node.
from.markdownDescription: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to the source value to move. Must match exactly one node.

to.description: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to the destination. The parent of the target should exist.
to.markdownDescription: A JSONPath Plus (https://www.npmjs.com/package/jsonpath-plus) query pointing to the destination. The parent of the target should exist.

args.description:
Move or rename a value from one location to another.

If `from` and `to` share the same parent the node will be renamed. Otherwise it will be relocated.
Only accept a single source and destination target.

Examples:
~~~yaml
# Rename a schema key
- command: move
  reason: "Normalize schema name to PascalCase"
  args:
    from: "$.components.schemas.user_response"
    to: "$.components.schemas.UserResponse"

# Move property between schemas
- command: move
  reason: "Relocate userId from Request to User schema"
  args:
    from: "$.components.schemas.Request.properties.userId"
    to: "$.components.schemas.User.properties.id"
~~~
args.markdownDescription:
Move or rename a value from one location to another.

If `from` and `to` share the same parent the node will be renamed. Otherwise it will be relocated.

    to: "$.components.schemas.User.properties.id"
~~~

code_samples.description:
Defines automatic snippet generation directly into your OpenAPI spec.

We support the following providers: Stainless (adds `x-stainless-snippets`), [ReadMe](https://readme.com/), [x-codeSamples](https://redocly.com/docs/api-reference-docs/specification-extensions/x-code-samples/), [Bump.sh](https://bump.sh/), [GitBook](https://www.gitbook.com/), [Redocly](https://redocly.com/), and [Mintlify](https://mintlify.com/) (the latter four are aliases for `x-codeSamples`).

See [our third-party docs integration docs](/docs/docs-integrations/#-enable-provider-compatible-code-samples-in-stainless) for more details.
code_samples.markdownDescription:
Defines automatic snippet generation directly into your OpenAPI spec.

We support the following providers: Stainless (adds `x-stainless-snippets`), [ReadMe](https://readme.com/), [x-codeSamples](https://redocly.com/docs/api-reference-docs/specification-extensions/x-code-samples/), [Bump.sh](https://bump.sh/), [GitBook](https://www.gitbook.com/), [Redocly](https://redocly.com/), and [Mintlify](https://mintlify.com/) (the latter four are aliases for `x-codeSamples`).

See [our third-party docs integration docs](/docs/docs-integrations/#-enable-provider-compatible-code-samples-in-stainless) for more details.

models.description:
Configure the models--named types--defined in the resource.

Each key in the object is the name of the model and the value
is either the name of a schema in `#/components/schemas` or an object with more detail.

openapi_uri.description:
`openapi_uri` is a reference to the OpenAPI definition of the schema, e.g. `#/components/schemas/Card` or `#/components/schemas/CardList/items`.

All `$ref` and indirect references to the schema this path points to will have the same canonical name and share a type definition.

For schemas under `#/components/schemas/*`, you can specify just the last path segment instead of the full JSON path (e.g. `Card` instead of `#/components/schemas/Card`).

If you need a more powerful way to match a schema, `openapi_uri` can also be a [JMESPath](https://jmespath.org/) or a [JSONPath-plus](https://jsonpath-plus.github.io/JSONPath/docs/ts/) query. This query should match exactly one schema, and not mutate or create new references.
openapi_uri.markdownDescription:
`openapi_uri` is a reference to the OpenAPI definition of the schema, e.g. `#/components/schemas/Card` or `#/components/schemas/CardList/items`.

All `$ref` and indirect references to the schema this path points to will have the same canonical name and share a type definition.

For schemas under `#/components/schemas/*`, you can specify just the last path segment instead of the full JSON path (e.g. `Card` instead of `#/components/schemas/Card`).

If you need a more powerful way to match a schema, `openapi_uri` can also be a [JMESPath](https://jmespath.org/) or a [JSONPath-plus](https://jsonpath-plus.github.io/JSONPath/docs/ts/) query. This query should match exactly one schema, and not mutate or create new references.

deduplicate.description:
Declaring a schema to be a model transforms all referentially-equal versions of that schema in the OpenAPI spec to be rendered as a reference, rather than re-declaring the definition.

This doesn't work if the schemas you want to reference the model are not referentially the same (i.e. a `$ref` in the OpenAPI spec).

`deduplicate` tells the Stainless generator to render the schemas listed on it to render them as a reference to the model. This is useful when you have schemas that are either exactly the same, but referentially not equal (e.g. not defined in `#/components/schemas/…` OR if the schemas are similar, but close enough that you would want them to be the same type in the generated SDK.

items.description: The reference path to the schema, e.g. `#/components/schemas/Card`, or the shorthand Card.
items.markdownDescription: The reference path to the schema, e.g. `#/components/schemas/Card`, or the shorthand Card.

deduplicate.markdownDescription:
Declaring a schema to be a model transforms all referentially-equal versions of that schema in the OpenAPI spec to be rendered as a reference, rather than re-declaring the definition.

This doesn't work if the schemas you want to reference the model are not referentially the same (i.e. a `$ref` in the OpenAPI spec).

`deduplicate` tells the Stainless generator to render the schemas listed on it to render them as a reference to the model. This is useful when you have schemas that are either exactly the same, but referentially not equal (e.g. not defined in `#/components/schemas/…` OR if the schemas are similar, but close enough that you would want them to be the same type in the generated SDK.

skip_test_reason.description:
Skip the generated unit test in all languages or particular languages with a given reason.

This will skip the test in all languages:

~~~yaml
skip_test_reason: "Requires body parameter not yet implement by test harness."
~~~

This will skip the test in a specific language (multiple languages also can be specified):
~~~yaml
skip_test_reason:
  ruby: "TODO: Investigate Ruby test failure."
~~~

~~~

body_param_name.markdownDescription: Parameter name to be used for the request parameters argument. Required when the request body is not an object (for example, an array).

positional_params.description:
When specified, this overrides the default behavior (to use the *last* path parameter as a positional parameter).

If a parameter is in this list, it is generated as a positional parameter in the given order.
If a parameter is not in this list, it is generated as a structured/named parameter.

method.description:
Define the type of method this endpoint represents.
- create: handles creation of the resource. Must be a POST
- read: handles reading of the resource. Must be a GET
- list: handles listing of the resource. Must be a GET
- update: handles updating of the resource. Must be a PUT or PATCH
- delete: handles deleting the resource. Must be a DELETE
- upsert: handles both updating and creating of the resource. Can be PUT or PATCH
- skip: don't include this method in generation
method.markdownDescription:
Define the type of method this endpoint represents.
- create: handles creation of the resource. Must be a POST
- read: handles reading of the resource. Must be a GET
- list: handles listing of the resource. Must be a GET
- update: handles updating of the resource. Must be a PUT or PATCH
- delete: handles deleting the resource. Must be a DELETE
- upsert: handles both updating and creating of the resource. Can be PUT or PATCH
- skip: don't include this method in generation

id_path_param.description: Defined the name of the path param that is used as the ID of the resource.

id_property.markdownDescription: Define the name of the property that represents the ID for the resource. It may exist as a path, body, or response parameter.

update_behavior.description:
Specify how the update method on the resource should encode the request body.
- put: The entire resource is replaced with the new resource.
- json_merge_patch: The resource is updated as a JSON Merge Patch, only providing changed fields.

If null, then json_merge_patch will be used for PATCH endpoints, and put otherwise.
update_behavior.markdownDescription:
Specify how the update method on the resource should encode the request body.
- put: The entire resource is replaced with the new resource.
- json_merge_patch: The resource is updated as a JSON Merge Patch, only providing changed fields.

If null, then json_merge_patch will be used for PATCH endpoints, and put otherwise.

deprecated.description:
Set the deprecation message for this method.

To set different deprecation messages for each language, provide an object with properties for each language and a `default` to use for unspecified languages.  For example:

~~~yaml
deprecated:
  typescript: "Use `fooBar` instead."
  go: "Use `FooBar` instead."
  default: "Use /v2/foo/bar instead."
~~~

To set the same deprecation message for all languages, provide only the deprecation message as a string:

~~~yaml
deprecated: "Use /v2/foo/bar instead."
~~~

deprecated.description:
Set the deprecation message for this method.

To set different deprecation messages for each language, provide an object with properties for each language and a `default` to use for unspecified languages.  For example:

~~~yaml
deprecated:
  typescript: "Use `fooBar` instead."
  go: "Use `FooBar` instead."
  default: "Use /v2/foo/bar instead."
~~~

To set the same deprecation message for all languages, provide only the deprecation message as a string:

~~~yaml
deprecated: "Use /v2/foo/bar instead."
~~~

methods.markdownDescription:
Configure the methods defined in this resource.

Each key in the object is the name of the method and the value
is either an endpoint (for example, `get /foo`) or an object with more detail.

description.markdownDescription: Add a docstring for the resource.

standalone_api.description:
If `true`, don't prefix any type names with this resource or its ancestors' names.

For example, consider an `external` resource with `customers` nested under it:

With `standalone_api` set to `true`, Go will generate an `ExternalCustomerListParams` type in the `requests` package.

With `standalone_api` set to `false`, Go will generate a `CustomerListParams` type in the `external_requests` package.

With `standalone_api` set to `false`, Go will generate a `CustomerListParams` type in the `external_requests` package.

You can override this in subresources or individual methods.

This doesn't affect the request-level timeout.

unsupported_delete_behavior.description:
Configures whether a warning message is shown to users when they attempt to delete a resource that
does not have a delete endpoint.

- warning: Does nothing upon delete, but generates a warning message before the user attempts to create or delete the resource.

- no_warning: Does nothing upon delete, and does not generate a warning message.

Configures whether a warning message is shown to users when they attempt to delete a resource that
does not have a delete endpoint.

- warning: Does nothing upon delete, but generates a warning message before the user attempts to create or delete the resource.

- no_warning: Does nothing upon delete, and does not generate a warning message.

version.description:
The schema version for this resource. This is used by Terraform to track schema changes
and perform state migrations when the schema evolves over time.

Only valid for resources, not data sources. Typically incremented by 1 for each
breaking schema change.

terraform.markdownDescription: Terraform configuration options for this resource.

use_namespace_in_type_names.description:
If `true`, always prefix parameters and unnamed response types with resource names.

If `false`, don't prefix parameters and unnamed response types with ancestor resource names.

If this property isn't present in your Stainless config, the default behavior for the language will be used (e.g., Go always prefixes while Node doesn't).

For example, consider the nested resource `/financial_accounts/balances`.  Set `use_namespace_in_type_names` to `true` to generate names like `FinancialAccountBalanceListParams`.

Now consider the nested resource `financial_accounts/financial_transactions`.  Set `use_namespace_in_type_names` to `false` to generate names like `FinancialTransactionGetResponse` without the `FinancialAccount` prefix.
use_namespace_in_type_names.markdownDescription:
If `true`, always prefix parameters and unnamed response types with resource names.

If `false`, don't prefix parameters and unnamed response types with ancestor resource names.

If this property isn't present in your Stainless config, the default behavior for the language will be used (e.g., Go always prefixes while Node doesn't).

For example, consider the nested resource `/financial_accounts/balances`.  Set `use_namespace_in_type_names` to `true` to generate names like `FinancialAccountBalanceListParams`.

Now consider the nested resource `financial_accounts/financial_transactions`.  Set `use_namespace_in_type_names` to `false` to generate names like `FinancialTransactionGetResponse` without the `FinancialAccount` prefix.

subresources.description:
Define nested namespaces for accessing models and methods.

For example, with a "cards" resource containing a "transactions" subresource, the
generated Python method invocation is `client.cards.transactions.retrieve()`.
subresources.markdownDescription:
Define nested namespaces for accessing models and methods.

generated Python method invocation is `client.cards.transactions.retrieve()`.
